extra.message: SQL injection vulnerability: Username query parameter is directly interpolated into SQL query using f-string without parameterization. Query: f'SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = "{username}";' allows attackers to inject arbitrary SQL code.

SQL injection vulnerability: All User model fields (name, username, password, address, email, contact) are directly interpolated into INSERT query using f-string without parameterization. Attacker-controlled values from request body are embedded directly into SQL: f'INSERT INTO users (...) VALUES ("{user.name}", "{user.username}", ...)'. No input sanitization or parameterized queries used.

SQL injection vulnerability: Username query parameter is directly interpolated into DELETE query using f-string without parameterization. Query: f'DELETE FROM users WHERE username = "{username}";' allows attackers to delete arbitrary rows or manipulate database.

SQL injection vulnerability: User.address field is directly interpolated into DELETE query without quotes or parameterization. Query: f'DELETE FROM users WHERE address = {user.address};' allows SQL injection. Even worse, the lack of quotes makes exploitation trivial.

NoSQL injection vulnerability: Raw JSON request body is passed directly to get_nosql_users() as a MontyDB query without validation. Attackers can inject MongoDB operators like {"username": {"$ne": ""}} or {"$where": "..."} to bypass filters, exfiltrate data, or manipulate queries. No query operator whitelist or sanitization.

Potential XSS vulnerability: app.title (Swagger UI HTML injection vector). While app.title is hardcoded in this case, the HTML response on line 263 concatenates app.title directly into HTML without escaping: 'title>' + app.title + ' - Swagger UI'. If app.title ever becomes user-controllable, this allows XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: Potential XSS vulnerability: app.title (ReDoc HTML injection vector). While app.title is hardcoded in this case, the HTML response on line 268 concatenates app.title directly into HTML without escaping: 'title>' + app.title + ' - ReDoc'. If app.title ever becomes user-controllable, this allows XSS.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: Missing authentication and authorization on /reset endpoint. The POST /reset endpoint allows unauthenticated users to completely reset the database by deleting all SQL and NoSQL data. This is a critical access control vulnerability that allows any attacker to perform destructive operations.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authentication and authorization on /select endpoint. Users can query the database without authentication to retrieve arbitrary user records including username, address, email, and contact information. No access control checks present.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authentication and authorization on /user endpoint (PUT). Users can create new records without authentication. The endpoint accepts any User object and inserts it directly into both SQL and NoSQL databases without verifying the requester's identity or authorization.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authentication and authorization on /user endpoint (DELETE). Unauthenticated users can delete arbitrary user records from the database. No authentication or authorization checks verify if the requester has permission to delete user accounts.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authentication and authorization on /find endpoint (GET). Unauthenticated users can query the NoSQL database to retrieve arbitrary user records by username without authentication or authorization controls.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authentication and authorization on /find endpoint (POST). Unauthenticated users can perform arbitrary NoSQL queries against the database. Combined with NoSQL injection vulnerability, this enables unrestricted data exfiltration.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
